showcases.label: L2 / 40 (6.18x speedup)
showcases.blurb: Seventeen separate best-score updates across 200 search steps. Never reaches MCTS's 10× reward ceiling, so the full trajectory plays without an auto-pause.

showcases.label: L2 / 83 (10+ speedup)
showcases.blurb: Sixteen distinct best-score updates. Climbs through the MCTS reward ceiling at step 25, then exploration keeps stumbling onto faster strategies all the way to a 912× speedup.

showcases.label: L2 / 84 (10+ speedup)
showcases.blurb: Plateaus around 5× through the first 140 steps; then a structural large step finally breaks out and reaches 22.4×. A clear example of diversity-preserving search paying off late.

showcases.label: L2 / 42 (10+ speedup)
showcases.blurb: Step 2 already hits 28× — well past MCTS's 10× reward ceiling. After that, MCTS no longer differentiates, yet exploration keeps stumbling onto faster strategies (87× → 125× → 249×).

showcases.label: L2 / 44 (10+ speedup)
showcases.blurb: Saturates at 10× around step 54, then continues climbing to 92×.

showcases.label: L2 / 18 (10+ speedup)
showcases.blurb: First correct kernel is already 15.65× — past the MCTS reward ceiling immediately. The rest of the run is exploration on top of a flat reward landscape, eventually finding 98×.

showcases.label: L2 / 63 (5.53x speedup)
showcases.blurb: Fourteen progress events spread across the full 200 steps without ever crossing 10× — the search stays inside MCTS's sensitive reward range the whole time.

showcases.label: L2 / 29 (5.05x speedup)
showcases.blurb: Eleven incremental improvements to a final speedup of 5.05×. Small-step refinement chains stitched together by occasional large-step branching.

showcases.label: L2 / 70 (5.98x speedup)
showcases.blurb: Ten progress events over 200 steps; ends just under the saturation point at 5.98×. The full trajectory plays without entering the saturation regime.

showcases.label: L2 / 92 (3.88x speedup)
showcases.blurb: First correct kernel already beats baseline (1.41×). Eleven jumps of incremental refinement to 3.88×, well below the saturation point — pure reward-driven optimization.
